narrative: We need to scale distributed manufacturing to support innovation and development in the US — ground systems infrastructure for aerospace, basic energy infrastructure, and cryogenic infrastructure for fusion and advanced energy.

items.headline: Sentinel ICBM program hit by software delays, Minuteman extension risks: GAO
items.one_liner: GAO reports software delays in the Sentinel ICBM program, which requires replacing over 600 facilities across five states.
items.caption: Replacing 600+ facilities across five states is one of the largest defense infrastructure buildouts in a generation — and it's already hitting delays. This is exactly why we need to invest in scalable, distributed construction and manufacturing capacity now. #DefenseIndustry #AerospaceMfg #Infrastructure

items.headline: Northrop not seeking direct DoD investment for solid rocket motors, but suppliers might: CEO
items.one_liner: Northrop Grumman's CEO says the company won't seek direct DoD investment for solid rocket motors but suppliers may need support.
items.caption: Northrop wants to own its solid rocket motor capacity — but the supplier base underneath still needs investment. Primes can't scale alone. Distributed manufacturing and supplier resilience are the real bottleneck. #DefenseIndustry #SupplyChain #AerospaceMfg

items.headline: Heron Power raises $140M to modernize electrical transformers
items.one_liner: Heron Power raised $140M to modernize electrical transformer manufacturing amid nationwide supply shortages.
items.caption: Transformers have been built the same way since the 1800s — and now they're in critical shortage as power demand surges. $140M into modernizing this manufacturing is a signal: basic energy infrastructure is a scaling problem, not just a technology problem. #EnergyInfrastructure #Manufacturing #SupplyChain

items.headline: Airbus Revises Commercial Aircraft Production Targets - Aviation Week
items.one_liner: Airbus has revised its commercial aircraft production targets amid ongoing manufacturing capacity challenges.
items.caption: Airbus adjusting production targets is a reminder that even the world's largest OEMs can't will capacity into existence. Scaling aerospace manufacturing requires distributed production infrastructure — not just ambition. #AerospaceMfg #SupplyChain #Aviation

items.headline: ATR targets growth with new assembly line though supply chain constraints linger - AeroTime
items.one_liner: ATR is building a new assembly line to support growth, though supply chain constraints continue to limit production.
items.caption: New assembly lines are necessary but not sufficient. ATR's supply chain constraints mirror what we see across aerospace: the bottleneck isn't final assembly — it's the distributed supplier base feeding it. #AerospaceMfg #SupplyChain #Manufacturing

items.headline: Silicon Valley is building a shadow power grid for data centers across the U.S. - The Washington Post
items.one_liner: Tech companies are building a parallel private power grid across the US to meet surging data center energy demand.
items.caption: Silicon Valley building its own shadow power grid is the clearest signal yet: existing US energy infrastructure can't keep pace with demand. We need to scale basic energy infrastructure — generation, transmission, cryogenic systems — with the same urgency we bring to software. #EnergyInfrastructure #Infrastructure #Manufacturing

items.headline: Dept. of Workforce Development: ICYMI: Gov. Evers announces Wisconsin won $7.3 million to fund new workforce training grants for advanced manufacturing and AI sectors - WisPolitics
items.one_liner: Wisconsin received $7.3M in federal grants for workforce training in advanced manufacturing and AI sectors.
items.caption: You can't scale distributed manufacturing without a distributed workforce. Wisconsin's $7.3M in advanced manufacturing training grants is a step — but the national need is orders of magnitude larger. #AdvancedManufacturing #Workforce #SupplyChain

items.headline: Agile Space Industries Oversubscribed $17M Series A Accelerates Growth of In-Space Propulsion Capabilities
items.one_liner: Agile Space Industries closed an oversubscribed $17M Series A to scale in-space chemical propulsion manufacturing.
items.caption: Agile Space raising $17M to scale propulsion manufacturing out of Durango, CO is exactly the model we need more of — distributed, specialized aerospace production outside traditional hubs. This is how you build a resilient industrial base. #AerospaceMfg #SpaceIndustry #Manufacturing

items.headline: China’s nuclear submarine production rate surpasses that of US: Report
items.one_liner: China launched 10 submarines totaling 79,000 tonnes from 2021–2025, surpassing US production rates per IISS research.
items.caption: China outpacing US submarine production isn't a technology gap — it's a manufacturing capacity gap. 10 submarines in four years vs. our current rate should be a wake-up call. Scaling defense industrial infrastructure is a national security imperative. #DefenseIndustry #SupplyChain #Manufacturing

items.headline: FirstEnergy 5-year spending plan jumps 30%, to $36B, driven by transmission
items.one_liner: FirstEnergy increased its five-year capital spending plan 30% to $36B, driven primarily by transmission infrastructure.
items.caption: FirstEnergy's $36B capital plan — up 30% — is driven by transmission buildout and new generation. This is the scale of investment US energy infrastructure demands. The question is whether our manufacturing base can deliver the hardware fast enough. #EnergyInfrastructure #Infrastructure #SupplyChain
